description:
You are working as a junior data-analyst who has been given a small CSV
    containing company staff information.  
    Your objectives are:

    1. Locate the input file  
       /home/user/data/employees.csv

    2. Produce a filtered & sorted CSV that only contains the rows that meet
       BOTH of these conditions  
         • department column equals the literal text Engineering  
         • salary column is strictly greater than 70000  

       a. The resulting file must be written to  
          /home/user/output/engineering_high_salary.csv

       b. The new file must keep exactly the same four-column header line
          (id,name,department,salary) in that order.  
       c. All qualifying rows must be ordered by the id column in ascending
          numeric order.  
       d. No extra blank lines, no additional quoting, and the column delimiter
          must remain a single comma.  
       e. Line-terminator must be Unix newline “\n”.

    3. Generate a plain-text log summarising what you did.  
       Write the log to  
       /home/user/output/analysis.log  

       The log must contain exactly three lines, **in this exact order**:

           Total_records_in_input:<N>
           Selected_records:<M>
           Average_salary_selected:<A>

       • <N> must be the number of data rows that were present in
         employees.csv (do NOT count the header).  
       • <M> must be the number of rows you wrote to
         engineering_high_salary.csv.  
       • <A> must be the arithmetic mean of the salary column of the selected
         rows, rounded to two decimal places.  
       • There must be no spaces before or after the colons and no thousands
         separators in the numbers.

    4. If /home/user/output/ does not yet exist, create it with default
       permissions (mkdir –p style).

    When you are done you should be able to run:

        cat /home/user/output/engineering_high_salary.csv
        cat /home/user/output/analysis.log

    and see data that matches the required format exactly.  
    Use any standard command-line tools (cut, grep, awk, csvkit, etc.) but
    keep the solution to a concise series of shell commands.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent starts):

      File: /home/user/data/employees.csv  
      Contents (7 data rows + 1 header, Unix newlines):

        id,name,department,salary
        1,John Smith,Engineering,90000
        2,Jane Doe,Marketing,65000
        3,Bob Johnson,Engineering,80000
        4,Alice Davis,HR,60000
        5,Charlie Brown,Engineering,72000
        6,Eve Black,Engineering,68000
        7,Frank White,Sales,70000

    Expected artefacts after successful completion:

    1. Directory /home/user/output/ exists.

    2. File /home/user/output/engineering_high_salary.csv  
       Exact contents:

        id,name,department,salary
        1,John Smith,Engineering,90000
        3,Bob Johnson,Engineering,80000
        5,Charlie Brown,Engineering,72000

       (3 data rows, sorted by id, no trailing blank line, Unix newlines)

    3. File /home/user/output/analysis.log  
       Exact contents (three lines, Unix newlines):

        Total_records_in_input:7
        Selected_records:3
        Average_salary_selected:80666.67
